evals.prompt: I have a DSPy ChainOfThought module that generates product descriptions, but sometimes they are too long or miss required keywords. I want it to automatically retry and improve until the description meets my quality bar. Show me how to use dspy.Refine for this.
evals.expected_output: A working dspy.Refine setup wrapping the ChainOfThought module with a graduated reward function that scores length and keyword presence, a threshold around 0.8, and N=3 to 5 attempts.

assertions.name: uses_dspy_refine
assertions.description: Uses dspy.Refine with module, N, reward_fn, and threshold parameters

assertions.name: reward_fn_signature
assertions.description: Reward function accepts (args, pred) and returns a float

assertions.name: graduated_scoring
assertions.description: Reward function uses graduated scoring (partial credit), not just 0 or 1

assertions.name: threshold_below_one
assertions.description: Threshold is set below 1.0 (e.g., 0.8) for a multi-criteria reward

assertions.name: provider_agnostic
assertions.description: LM configuration uses provider-agnostic format with alternative comment

assertions.name: no_lm_in_reward
assertions.description: Does not put LM calls inside the reward function

evals.prompt: What is the difference between dspy.Refine and dspy.BestOfN? I want to generate multiple candidate answers and pick the best one.
evals.expected_output: A comparison explaining that Refine generates feedback from failures for sequential improvement while BestOfN runs independent attempts. Recommends Refine when feedback helps and BestOfN when attempts are independent.

assertions.name: refine_has_feedback
assertions.description: Explains that Refine generates feedback from failures and feeds it into subsequent attempts

assertions.name: bestofn_independent
assertions.description: Explains that BestOfN runs independent attempts with no cross-attempt learning

assertions.name: both_support_threshold
assertions.description: Notes that both support early stopping via threshold

assertions.name: refine_for_feedback
assertions.description: Recommends Refine for tasks where feedback improves later attempts

assertions.name: bestofn_for_diversity
assertions.description: Recommends BestOfN for sampling diversity or independent attempts

assertions.name: no_bestofn_misconception
assertions.description: Does not incorrectly claim BestOfN always runs all N attempts

evals.prompt: I want to use dspy.Refine to validate that my AI generates valid JSON. Build me a simple example.
evals.expected_output: A Refine wrapper with a reward function that parses JSON and returns 1.0 for valid JSON, 0.0 for invalid. Uses threshold=1.0 since JSON validity is binary.

assertions.name: wraps_module
assertions.description: Uses dspy.Refine wrapping a Predict or ChainOfThought module

assertions.name: json_loads_validation
assertions.description: Reward function uses json.loads to validate

assertions.name: binary_reward
assertions.description: Returns 1.0 for valid JSON and 0.0 for invalid

assertions.name: threshold_one_for_binary
assertions.description: Threshold is set to 1.0 since this is a binary criterion

assertions.name: reasonable_n
assertions.description: N is set to a reasonable value (2-5)

assertions.name: cot_inner_module
assertions.description: Inner module is ChainOfThought rather than bare Predict when reasoning helps
